title: Workflow Functionality
excerpt: It would be helpful if we had the ability to reorder steps inside a workflow map. Currently our company utilizes several large workflows to assist in several areas. The ability to reorder the steps if needed would be gre…

posts.author: KristaBowman (Krista Bowman)
posts.cookedHtml:
It would be helpful if we had the ability to reorder steps inside a workflow map. Currently our company utilizes several large workflows to assist in several areas. The ability to reorder the steps if needed would be great.

We would also benefit from the ability to copy/paste sections of the workflow and add additional connectors. This would allow us to stop duplicating steps in the process that may occur regardless of other previous steps, or that may occur in several different workflows.

Along the same lines, it would be beneficial to have the ability to add additional triggers to a workflow opposed to having to duplicate them.

As we use a lot of workflows it would be beneficial to have the ability to add a workflow inside of the folder in a workspace.

posts.author: kasperhusted (Kasper Husted)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes, this would be very useful.

I also had a case recently, where I needed to move a branch form a No to a Yes after an If/else condition, which turned out to be impossible without removing the entire branch and recreating it from scratch.

posts.author: KristaBowman (Krista Bowman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Kasper,
We also had this same issue. We needed to add the if/else condition to the beginning of a flow with a couple hundred steps. It has been a point of contention.
